Package Dungeon_Classes

Class Tile

java.lang.Object
Dungeon_Classes.Tile
Direct Known Subclasses:
DestructibleTile
public class Tile extends Object
Represents a fundamental structural unit or coordinate node within the dungeon grid map. Tracks individual positioning coordinates, symbol identifiers, passability metrics, breakable traits, and environmental hazard damage weights.
Author:
Katigbak and Porciuncula

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected float
    damage
    The value of environmental hazard damage dealt to a character upon entering.
    protected boolean
    destructible
    Flag identifying whether this map component can be broken down or collected.
    protected boolean
    passable
    Flag identifying whether an entity can freely step onto this coordinate node.
    protected char
    symbol
    The single character indicator tracking the structural identity layout.
    protected int
    x
    The horizontal grid row index of the tile.
    protected int
    y
    The vertical grid column index of the tile.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Tile(int x, int y, char symbol)
    Constructs a tile element setting up specific grid values, map representation characters, and initializes its movement property configurations.
    Tile(Tile refTile)
    Constructs a new tile by copying attributes and values out of an existing reference node.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    assignColor()
    Evaluates the active character symbol signature to supply a matching ANSI text formatting escape color code for terminal output rendering.
    void
    assignProperties()
    Configures the path passability, modification criteria, and default floor hazard damage metrics by resolving structural parameters mapped to individual entity symbols.
    float
    getDamage()
    Retrieves the value of environmental penalty damage associated with this element.

    Retrieves the visual symbol character identifier of this tile.

    Retrieves the row position index of this tile.

    Retrieves the column position index of this tile.

    Checks if the node can be broken or extracted.
    boolean

    Checks if characters can move across this space.
    void
    setDamage(float damage)
    Updates the penalty scale mapping to this node.
    void

    Updates the modification constraint status fro this tile.
    void
    setPassable(boolean passable)
    Updates the path verification flag state for this tile.
    void
    setSymbol(char symbol)
    Alters the visual symbol configuration and triggers a state property reassignment.
    void
    setX(int x)
    Updates the row position index of this tile.
    void
    setY(int y)
    Updates the column position index of this tile.

  • Field Details

    • x

      protected int x
      The horizontal grid row index of the tile.

    • y

      protected int y
      The vertical grid column index of the tile.

    • symbol

      protected char symbol
      The single character indicator tracking the structural identity layout.

    • passable

      protected boolean passable
      Flag identifying whether an entity can freely step onto this coordinate node.

      protected boolean destructible
      Flag identifying whether this map component can be broken down or collected.

      protected float damage
      The value of environmental hazard damage dealt to a character upon entering.

  • Constructor Details

    • Tile

      public Tile(int x, int y, char symbol)
      Constructs a tile element setting up specific grid values, map representation characters, and initializes its movement property configurations.
      Parameters:
      x - the horizontal row coordinate position
      y - the vertical column coordinate position
      symbol - the character symbol defining the node template type

    • Tile

      public Tile(Tile refTile)
      Constructs a new tile by copying attributes and values out of an existing reference node.
      Parameters:
      refTile - the source structural tile instance to duplicate

  • Method Details

    • getX

      public int getX()
      Retrieves the row position index of this tile.
      Returns:
      the row position index

    • setX

      public void setX(int x)
      Updates the row position index of this tile.
      Parameters:
      x - the new row position index to apply

    • getY

      public int getY()
      Retrieves the column position index of this tile.
      Returns:
      the column position index

    • setY

      public void setY(int y)
      Updates the column position index of this tile.
      Parameters:
      y - the new column position index to apply

    • getSymbol

      public char getSymbol()
      Retrieves the visual symbol character identifier of this tile.
      Returns:
      the visual symbol character identifier

    • setSymbol

      public void setSymbol(char symbol)
      Alters the visual symbol configuration and triggers a state property reassignment.
      Parameters:
      symbol - the replacement type character assignment

    • isPassable

      public boolean isPassable()
      Checks if characters can move across this space.
      Returns:
      true if characters can move across this space, false otherwise

    • setPassable

      public void setPassable(boolean passable)
      Updates the path verification flag state for this tile.
      Parameters:
      passable - the new path verification flag state

    • isDestructible

      public boolean isDestructible()
      Checks if the node can be broken or extracted.
      Returns:
      true if the node can be broken or extracted, false otherwise

    • setDestructible

      public void setDestructible(boolean destructible)
      Updates the modification constraint status fro this tile.
      Parameters:
      destructible - the new modification constraint status

    • getDamage

      public float getDamage()
      Retrieves the value of environmental penalty damage associated with this element.
      Returns:
      the value of environmental penalty damage associated with this element

    • setDamage

      public void setDamage(float damage)
      Updates the penalty scale mapping to this node.
      Parameters:
      damage - the updated penalty scale mapping to this node

    • assignColor

      public String assignColor()
      Evaluates the active character symbol signature to supply a matching ANSI text formatting escape color code for terminal output rendering.
      Returns:
      a string tracking the target terminal layout formatting color sequence

    • assignProperties

      public void assignProperties()
      Configures the path passability, modification criteria, and default floor hazard damage metrics by resolving structural parameters mapped to individual entity symbols.
